summary:
The main cause of a Jersey finger injury involves rupturing the flexor digitorum profundus tendon where it attaches near the end bone of fingers commonly affected due to specific stresses experienced while playing contact sports such as rugby or football. This highlights why these tendons are prone to tearing under forceful hyperextension motions characteristic of those activities. Sentence count: 2
To improve conciseness without losing essential information, I'll refine further focusing strictly on core elements needed here.

Revised Summary: A Jersey finger results from the rupture of the flexor digitorum profundus tendon primarily affecting middle and ring fingers when they experience sudden forced extension common in high-contact sports. Key factors include repetitive strain and acute trauma leading directly to tendon failure at its insertion site.
